value.found_quote:
Starting with `gpt-image-1` and later models, the [Image API](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/api-reference/images) provides two endpoints, each with distinct capabilities:

- **Generations**: [Generate images](#generate-images) from scratch based on a text prompt
- **Edits**: [Modify existing images](#edit-images) using a new prompt, either partially or entirely

...

The Responses API and Image API support streaming image generation. You can stream partial images as the APIs generate them, providing a more interactive experience.
value.rationale: The page clearly describes the Image API as a dedicated API for generating and editing images from text prompts using GPT Image models (e.g., gpt-image-2). It also explicitly states that the Image API supports streaming of partial images, with code examples showing `stream: true` and `partial_images: 2` parameters. All key aspects of the claim — dedicated Image API, text-prompt-based generation and editing, GPT Image models, and progressive streaming of partial images — are substantiated by the page.
